subject: [PATCH v5 10/11] mm: install PMD swap entries on swap-out

2026-07-22.patch_summary: This patch optimizes the swap-out process for huge pages (Transparent Huge Pages, or THPs) by installing PMD-level swap entries instead of splitting them into individual PTEs. This reduces overhead and preserves the original mapping across the swap round-trip. The patch introduces a new counter `thp_swpout_pmd` to track PMD mappings replaced by PMD-level swap entries, with documentation noting that this counter can be incremented multiple times for fork-shared THPs.

reviews.author: Matthew Wilcox
reviews.summary: The reviewer expressed concern about adding complexity to try_to_unmap() and suggested removing TTU_SPLIT_HUGE_PMD entirely, instead handling folios of any size and splitting them as needed.
reviews.raw_body:
This makes me sad.  It feels like we're bolting more complexity onto
try_to_unmap() instead of removing TTU_SPLIT_HUGE_PMD entirely.

Ideally we'd just cope with folios of whatever size (including
PMD and PUD mapped folios) and only handle hugetlb weirdness when we
absolutely have to.  Similarly, we could split PUDs to PMDs and
PMDs to PTEs when we need to, without the caller specifying
TTU_SPLIT_anything.

Is there a reason we can't do that?

2026-07-23.patch_summary: This patch optimizes the swap-out process for huge pages (Transparent Huge Pages, or THPs) by installing PMD-level swap entries instead of splitting them into individual PTEs. This reduces overhead and preserves the huge mapping across the swap round-trip. The patch mirrors the existing PTE swap-out path at PMD granularity, handling zswap and adjusting MM_ANONPAGES / MM_SWAPENTS accordingly. It also introduces a new counter, thp_swpout_pmd, to track PMD mappings replaced by PMD-level swap entries.
